steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$f \left(x\right)={ \left(x+7\right)}^{2}+4$$Graph it and use the visible intercept, root, or vertex the question asks for. The requested value is $-7$.

steps.title: Recognize vertex form
steps.content: $f(x) = (x+7)^2 + 4$ is in vertex form $a(x-h)^2 + k$ with $a = 1$, $h = -7$, $k = 4$.

steps.title: Determine where the minimum occurs
steps.content: Since $a \gt  0$, the parabola opens upward and has its minimum at the vertex $x = h$.

steps.title: Compute the answer
steps.content: The minimum occurs at $x = -7$. The answer is -7.
